audit_status.source_base: Greek Galatians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for restoration, burden-bearing, sowing/reaping, doing good, circumcision boasting, cross, new creation, marks of Jesus.

verses.restored: Siblings, even if a human is overtaken in some misstep, you who are spiritual restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness, watching yourself lest you also be tested.
Bear one another's burdens, and in this way fulfill the Law of Messiah.
For if anyone thinks oneself to be something, being nothing, one deceives oneself.
But let each test one's own work, and then one will have the boast in oneself alone and not in another.
For each will bear one's own load.
Let the one being instructed in the word share in all good things with the one instructing.
Do not be deceived; the Divine is not mocked. For whatever a human sows, this also one will reap.
Because the one sowing into one's own flesh will reap decay from the flesh, but the one sowing into the Spirit will reap age-long life from the Spirit.
And let us not grow weary in doing the beautiful/good, for in proper season we will reap, not fainting.
So then, as we have season, let us work the good toward all, and especially toward those of the household of trust.
See with what large letters I wrote to you by my own hand.
As many as want to make a good face in flesh, these compel you to be circumcised, only so that they may not be pursued/persecuted for the cross of Messiah.
For not even those being circumcised themselves keep Law, but they want you to be circumcised so that in your flesh they may boast.
But may it not be for me to boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Messiah, through whom the world has been crucified to me and I to the world.
For neither circumcision nor uncircumcision is anything, but new creation.
And as many as will walk by this rule, peace upon them and mercy, and upon the Israel of the Divine.
From now on let no one give me troubles, for I carry the marks of Jesus in my body.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Messiah be with your spirit, siblings. Amen.

translation_notes: Bearing one another's burdens and each bearing one's own load are complementary: communal care and personal responsibility.
New creation in 6:15 is the final interpretive anchor over circumcision/uncircumcision.
Israel of the Divine in 6:16 should be handled carefully without supersessionist certainty; preserve ambiguity.
